questions.question: Numbers-driven check for Retail Sales Associate work using Customer service while handling 'Assist shoppers': Food cost £4.50, selling price £14. What is gross food margin %?
questions.why_asked: Tests numerical and analytical competence in Customer service — essential for Retail Sales Associate roles.

calculation.prompt: Food cost £4.50, selling price £14. What is gross food margin %?
calculation.answer: Margin = (14 − 4.50) / 14 × 100 = 67.9%. Monitor against target; if labour is high, contribution still may be insufficient.

evaluation_criteria: (Price − cost) / price
Express as %
Link to total GP
common_mistakes: Unit conversion errors (kN vs N, mg vs g).
Using wrong formula for support/loading conditions.
Quoting tabulated values without verifying edition/date of standard.

questions.question: What are the core operating principles and standard workflow for applying Merchandising in a Retail Sales Associate role while handling 'Assist shoppers'?
questions.why_asked: Tests whether you know how Merchandising is actually executed to standard, not only what it is called.
evaluation_criteria: Measurement design quality determines campaign learning quality.
Lead quality matters more than top-of-funnel volume.
Consistency between messaging and landing experience lifts conversion.
common_mistakes: Skipping verification or documentation steps.
Applying method without checking prerequisites.
Treating principles as optional under time pressure.
questions.model_answer:
Core principles for Merchandising as a Retail Sales Associate:

1. Measurement design quality determines campaign learning quality.
2. Lead quality matters more than top-of-funnel volume.
3. Consistency between messaging and landing experience lifts conversion.

Standard workflow:
1. Define ICP/audience segment and objective metric (pipeline, ROAS, conversion).
2. Design offer/message variant and channel plan.
3. Instrument tracking (UTM, attribution, CRM stage rules).
4. Run controlled test and analyze cohort performance.
5. Scale winning variant and archive learnings.

questions.question: Numbers-driven check for Retail Sales Associate work using Merchandising while handling 'Assist shoppers': Food cost £4.50, selling price £14. What is gross food margin %?
questions.why_asked: Tests numerical and analytical competence in Merchandising — essential for Retail Sales Associate roles.

calculation.prompt: Food cost £4.50, selling price £14. What is gross food margin %?
calculation.answer: Margin = (14 − 4.50) / 14 × 100 = 67.9%. Monitor against target; if labour is high, contribution still may be insufficient.

evaluation_criteria: (Price − cost) / price
Express as %
Link to total GP
common_mistakes: Unit conversion errors (kN vs N, mg vs g).
Using wrong formula for support/loading conditions.
Quoting tabulated values without verifying edition/date of standard.
questions.model_answer:
Problem: Food cost £4.50, selling price £14. What is gross food margin %?

Working:
1. (Price − cost) / price
2. Express as %
3. Link to total GP

Answer: Margin = (14 − 4.50) / 14 × 100 = 67.9%. Monitor against target; if labour is high, contribution still may be insufficient.

In Retail Sales Associate practice, I anchor this using: (Price − cost) / price, Express as %, Given data.
